_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — TIER BREAK from Tier 2 (Footman) to Tier 3 (Sergeant). Take the L6 'Veteran Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L7 'Footman Sergeant' variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Footman Sergeant (Tier 3 entry) = 5 visible adds (open-face steel sallet helm worn OVER mail coif, reinforced cuirass with 3 horizontal lames articulating the abdomen, steel gauntlets replacing bare hands with vambraces extending INTO the gauntlets, FLANGED STEEL mace head REPLACING the wooden mace head with 6 vertical flanges and slightly larger size, larger round shield with embossed sun-burst boss and ~10% larger diameter). Tier-break composite will use --dilate 20 (largest edit area in the chain). Within-tier chained input strategy: two inputs = L6 composite + L1_Base scale anchor. Major changes at L7: face becomes framed by the helm brim (still fully visible), mace head transitions wood → steel, hands become steel-clad. Silhouette mightiness rule active: shoulder envelope already maxed at L6, this level expands the helm vertically (helm dome adds ~1 inch of vertical extent to the silhouette) and grows the shield ~10% in diameter.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — CUIRASS articulation reduced from 3 horizontal lames to 1 SIMPLE HORIZONTAL SEAM + raised medial ridge per AD detail-economy ('rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); the multi-lame articulated banding pass was reading as fragmented surface detail. SALLET HELM is now framed as REPLACING the L3 steel skullcap (it is the FIRST FULL HELM, not the FIRST METAL HEADPIECE — that schedule moved to L3 per AD 'helmet much earlier'). Updated CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION: removed knife-sheath line (dropped per AD); added L3 skullcap line (now removed at L7 as the sallet replaces it) + L4 iron toe-cap + L5 full bicep-to-wrist scale mail + 2-wider-lame tassets + L6 ankle plates (not full sabatons) lines. Negatives updated: lock out 3-lame articulated cuirass, lock out narrow multi-lame tassets, lock out full sabatons (still arrive at L8).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v5 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L2 rim-rivet PRESERVE/negative refs — L2 IRON RIM RIVETS positive add was dropped; downstream shield-rim language no longer references L2 rivets.

v6 (2026-05-07): drop "~16 dark-iron studs around the rim band" from L7 shield add — surface-ornament accumulation that violates AD detail-economy and was likely amplifying the rivet/stud bleed-through pattern across the chain. L7 shield upgrade is now silhouette growth + embossed sun-burst boss only, no rim studs.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.
_meta.tier: Tier 3 — Footman Sergeant (TIER BREAK from Tier 2)

adds.summary: REPLACE the L3 low-profile steel skullcap with a much larger cool silver-gray polished steel domed sallet helm covering the top and sides of the head.

adds.title: REINFORCED CUIRASS
adds.summary: RAISED MEDIAL RIDGE PLUS A SINGLE SIMPLE HORIZONTAL SEAM (per AD detail-economy 'rely on shapes and silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree' — reduced from 3 articulated horizontal lames to ONE simple seam) — modify the existing full breastplate so its medial ridge running...

adds.summary: add articulated steel finger-plates and a steel knuckle-plate over the previously bare hands.

adds.title: FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD REPLACING THE WOODEN HEAD
adds.summary: REPLACE the wooden bulbous mace head (with its iron banding rings, six iron spikes, and steel reinforcement plate) with a SOLID STEEL FLANGED MACE HEAD.

adds.title: LARGER ROUND SHIELD WITH EMBOSSED SUN-BURST BOSS
adds.summary: expand the existing steel-faced round shield by approximately 10% in diameter (silhouette mightiness rule — visibly larger than at L6).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L6/composite/infantry_L6_v2_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Veteran Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every + + + + feature shown in the input image: (a) the FULL STEEL BREASTPLATE covering the entire torso from collarbone (where it joins the bevor) down to the leather belt at the waist; the breastplate has a slight medial ridge (the new step ADDS articulated horizontal lames across the abdomen — the breastplate is not removed); (b) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle (visible between the tassets at the front); (c) the STEEL TASSETS hanging from the belt at both hips — exactly 2 WIDER LAMES per tasset (NOT narrow multi-lames) with slight outward flare and visible dark-iron rivets at the top edge — the LIVERY tunic skirt remains visible as a narrow red+mustard band through the front gap between the two tassets; (d) the LAYERED 2-DISC STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (the new step KEEPS the 2-disc layering — 3-lame pauldrons arrive at this level); (e) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms sitting ON TOP of the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves (the new gauntlets EXTEND from these vambraces continuously — vambraces are not removed); (f) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (visible at upper edge of right tasset) — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath, NO second pouch; (g) the existing FULL BICEP-TO-WRIST SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES on both arms — chunky overlapping rounded scale plates covering the entire arm from shoulder seam to wrist with vambraces overlaying wrist-to-mid-forearm; NO exposed bicep / elbow / upper-forearm gap; (h) the LEATHER GRIP CORD wrapping the upper third of the mace haft (the new mace head replaces the wooden head only; the haft and grip cord remain unchanged); (i) the WARM-BROWN WOODEN MACE HAFT with the IRON REINFORCEMENT STRIP running along its length and three rivets — haft and strip remain unchanged at this level (only the mace HEAD changes from wood+spikes to flanged steel); (j) the STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with central steel boss, four iron cross strap-bands, continuous iron rim band; (k) the existing IRON TOE-CAP STRIP on the FRONT of each boot AND the existing STEEL ANKLE PLATE wrapping each boot at the ankle — at the boot armor is still iron toe-cap + steel ankle plate ONLY, with brown leather visible across the central top of the foot between them (full continuous sabatons arrive at this level, NOT yet at this level). DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the TIER BREAK from Tier 2 Footman to Tier 3 Sergeant. The unit transitions from leather-trim plate to articulated full plate; the mace head transitions from wooden to flanged steel; the hands become fully metalled; the head gains a steel sallet helm worn over the mail coif. Silhouette mightiness rule: the helm adds vertical extent at the head and the shield grows in diameter. (1) OPEN-FACE STEEL SALLET HELM REPLACING THE STEEL SKULLCAP (FIRST FULL HELM — the existing skullcap was the first metal headpiece; this sallet is the first FULL HELM, a clear VOLUME upgrade from the low-profile skullcap) — REPLACE the existing low-profile steel skullcap with a much larger cool silver-gray polished steel domed sallet helm covering the top and sides of the head. The sallet is rounded at the dome, has a subtle crest ridge running front-to-back along the top, and projects forward by approximately 1.5 inches over the brow as a flat brim that casts a soft shadow over the upper face. The helm has visible dark-iron rivets along the brim seam (~6 rivets evenly spaced across the front brim). The skullcap (small steel dome at the top + thin brow rim band inside the coif's facial opening) is FULLY GONE — replaced by the larger sallet which sits OVER the mail coif at the top of the head. The helm reads visibly LARGER and TALLER than the the levels above skullcap — the silhouette mightiness rule applies: this upgrade is volume, not just material. The helm DOES NOT have a visor, half-visor, or any face cover — the FACE remains FULLY VISIBLE below the brim: the eyes, nose, mustache, and beard are all clearly visible under the brim's shadow. The mail coif's BACK-OF-NECK and SHOULDER drape remains visible behind and below the helm — the helm sits OVER the coif and the coif drape continues from the lower edge of the helm down to the breastplate's collar line at the back and sides. The brim shape is NOT a wide kettle-helm brim — it is a forward-projecting sallet brim, broader at the front than at the sides. (2) REINFORCED CUIRASS — RAISED MEDIAL RIDGE PLUS A SINGLE SIMPLE HORIZONTAL SEAM — modify the existing full breastplate so its medial ridge running vertically down the center from collar to belt becomes thicker and more pronounced (a raised central ridge that catches a soft top-down highlight along its crest). Add ONE single simple horizontal seam across the abdomen at roughly the bottom-of-sternum line — a thin shadow seam (~1 line, not a stack of lames) suggesting that the cuirass has an upper plate (collar to seam) and a lower plate (seam to belt) joined at this seam. The cuirass otherwise reads as TWO smooth pieces joined at one seam — NOT three or four articulated horizontal lames stacked across the abdomen. CRITICAL: do NOT break the abdomen into multiple stacked horizontal bands; ONE simple seam only at the bottom-of-sternum line. The volume upgrade comes from the raised medial ridge (vertical depth) plus this single seam (subtle horizontal articulation), not from multi-lame fragmentation. (3) STEEL GAUNTLETS REPLACING THE BARE HANDS — add articulated steel finger-plates and a steel knuckle-plate over the previously bare hands. The vambraces on both forearms now EXTEND continuously into gauntlets at the wrist (no break in the metal between forearm and hand). Each gauntlet has: (i) a steel knuckle plate covering the back of the hand and knuckles, with four small dark-iron rivets at the knuckle line; (ii) articulated steel finger plates covering each of the four fingers and the thumb, with two small steel segments per finger to suggest articulated finger joints. The gauntlets are cool silver-gray polished steel matching the vambraces. The fingers of the anatomical-RIGHT gauntlet wrap around the upper haft of the mace just below the (new flanged steel) mace head; the anatomical-LEFT gauntlet remains hidden behind the round shield. The bare warm peachy-tan skin of the hands is FULLY GONE — no skin visible on either hand. (4) FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD REPLACING THE WOODEN HEAD — REPLACE the wooden bulbous mace head (with its iron banding rings, six iron spikes, and steel reinforcement plate) with a SOLID STEEL FLANGED MACE HEAD. The new flanged head is cool silver-gray polished steel with SIX VERTICAL FLANGES (raised steel ribs running from the top of the head to the bottom of the head, evenly spaced around the head's circumference). The flanges are sharp-edged steel ribs, each roughly half an inch wide and projecting outward from the central steel core by approximately 1 inch. The head is approximately 10-15% LARGER than the previous wooden head (silhouette mightiness rule — the head reads visibly bulkier than at this level). The wooden bulbous shape and the six iron spikes are FULLY REPLACED — no wood visible in the head, no spikes (the spikes are replaced by the flanges). The iron banding rings and reinforcement plate are FULLY GONE (replaced by the new steel flanged head). The wooden haft (with leather grip cord and iron reinforcement strip) remains unchanged below the head. (5) LARGER ROUND SHIELD WITH EMBOSSED SUN-BURST BOSS — expand the existing steel-faced round shield by approximately 10% in diameter (silhouette mightiness rule — visibly larger than at this level). The central steel boss is now embossed with a SUN-BURST PATTERN: a raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays radiating outward from the dome, each ray approximately 1.5 inches long, all rendered in cool silver-gray steel as raised relief detail. The four iron cross strap-bands and the continuous iron rim band remain. The shield's flat face is still cool silver-gray polished steel; the new sun-burst boss is the dominant ornament at the center. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (now framed at the top by the new helm brim, at the bottom by the bevor / upper-chest steel) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; the mail coif's back-of-neck and shoulder drape (the new helm sits OVER the coif at the top of the head); the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front gap between the two tassets; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves on both arms (no exposed bicep gap); the layered 2-disc steel pauldrons on both shoulders; the steel vambraces on both forearms (extending continuously into the new gauntlets); the leather grip cord on the upper haft; the iron reinforcement strip on the haft; the wooden mace haft (head replaced per Edit Goal item 4; haft remains wooden); the steel-faced round shield (now larger and with embossed boss per Edit Goal item 5); the existing iron toe-cap strip on the front of each boot AND the existing steel ankle plate at each ankle (no continuous full sabaton at this level — that arrives at this level); the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf below the tassets; the warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff visible at calf, heel, and central top of foot between the toe-cap and ankle plate. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, gauntleted hand grips the upper haft just below the new flanged mace head, mace head dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle, gauntleted hand hidden behind the shield. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin (face only — hands now metal), saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of helm dome at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (the new helm replaces the previous mail-coif top in the silhouette envelope but the silhouette top is at the same vertical position). BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the face — the existing sallet helm is OPEN-FACE only; eyes, nose, mustache, and beard remain fully visible under the brim's shadow; DO NOT add a half-visor, closed visor, eye-slit visor, or any face cover (half-visor arrives at this level, closed visor at this level); DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or any vertical helm extension other than the subtle dome ridge (horns arrive at this level, central crest at this level); DO NOT remove the mail coif's back-of-neck drape — the coif drape stays visible behind and below the new helm; DO NOT cover the entire head with mail — the new helm sits over the coif at the top, the coif drape remains at the back of the neck and shoulders; DO NOT make the helm a wide kettle-helm with a brim wider than the dome — it is a sallet (forward-projecting brim, dome wider than the brim sides); DO NOT make the gauntlets and the vambraces separate pieces with a visible seam between them — they are continuous metal from forearm to fingertip; DO NOT leave any bare skin on the hands — both hands FULLY metalled with articulated finger-plates and knuckle-plates; DO NOT add multiple horizontal lames across the abdomen of the cuirass — at the cuirass has ONE simple horizontal seam at the bottom-of-sternum line PLUS a raised vertical medial ridge, NOT 3 articulated lames (3-lame articulated banding was removed 2026-05-07); DO NOT break the cuirass into 3 / 4 / multi-lame stacked plates across the abdomen; DO NOT add engraved scrollwork / filigree / etched motifs on the cuirass at this level (engraving arrives at if at all per the level plan, and even then is being audited); DO NOT add scale-mail across the cuirass — the cuirass surface is smooth steel plate; DO NOT extend tassets longer than at this level — tasset extension to mid-thigh arrives at this level; DO NOT make each tasset more than 2 wider lames at this level — exactly 2 wider rectangular lames per tasset (NOT 3 narrow lames); DO NOT add full continuous toe+foot+ankle sabatons at this level — boot armor at is the existing iron toe-cap + steel ankle plate ONLY (full sabatons arrive at this level); DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, scabbard, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch is the ONLY belt accessory; DO NOT keep the existing small skullcap visible at the top of the head — the existing skullcap is REPLACED by the new larger sallet helm at this level (the upgrade is volume — full sallet vs low skullcap); DO NOT shrink the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves — sleeves remain full-length under the vambraces with no exposed bicep gap; DO NOT add a third or fourth disc to the pauldrons — they remain layered 2-disc at this level; DO NOT add gold rivets, gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or filigree; DO NOT make the flanged mace head a bulbous shape — the new mace head is a CYLINDRICAL/STAR-SHAPED solid steel head with 6 vertical flanges, NOT bulbous; DO NOT keep the iron banding rings or the wooden bulb shape on the mace head — those are FULLY REPLACED by the flanged steel head; DO NOT add spikes to the new flanged mace head — flanges replace spikes; the flanges have sharp edges but are not pointed spikes; DO NOT make the flanged mace head smaller than the previous wooden head — it is ~10-15% LARGER (silhouette mightiness rule); DO NOT remove the leather grip cord on the upper haft; DO NOT remove the iron reinforcement strip on the haft; DO NOT replace the wooden haft with metal — the haft remains warm-brown wood at this level; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT shrink the shield diameter — it grows ~10% larger at this level; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the boss remains cool silver-gray steel (gold boss embellishment arrives at this level); DO NOT add a cross emblem on the shield face — that arrives at this level; DO NOT remove the iron rim band, rim rivets, or cross strap-bands; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
